latest.title: Forests can cool the air below them
latest.script_key: forests can cool the air below them watch the shade and mist because leaves block direct sun and release water vapor that combination can make the forest floor feel like a different climate the canopy is a cooling machine not just cover which forest layer next

latest.title: Rock layers store ancient environments in stripes
latest.script_key: rock layers are time stamps made of stone watch the stripe pattern because each layer can mark a different setting river mud ocean floor windblown sand or volcanic ash stack enough layers and the cliff becomes a timeline which rock clue should we read next

latest.title: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick
latest.script_key: snakes sample the air with a tongue flick watch the tongue flick because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth that helps snakes track prey mates and danger without needing a loud chase the air is information would you notice the trail

latest.title: Snakes can sense heat when light disappears
latest.script_key: snakes can sense heat when light disappears watch the head before the strike because some snakes have heat sensitive pits that detect warm bodies that gives them a second kind of vision at night the target glows to them without glowing to us which reptile sense next

latest.title: Sheep recognize faces across the flock
latest.script_key: sheep can remember familiar faces watch the face and spacing because sheep can learn who is familiar in the flock that recognition helps them stay near safe partners and avoid stress the group is not just a blur to them which farm animal should we compare next

latest.title: The moon keeps one face turned toward Earth
latest.script_key: the moon shows us one face for a reason watch the moon s position because its rotation and orbit are locked together it spins once in about the same time it circles earth so the same side keeps facing us that is synchronized motion not stillness which orbit clue next

latest.title: Crystals build shape from repeating atoms
latest.script_key: crystals build shape from repeating atoms watch the edge grow because atoms or molecules lock into a repeating arrangement once the pattern starts new pieces fit most easily along the same order that is why a crystal can look designed which reaction should we slow down next

latest.title: Fossils turn old bones into time clues
latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: Auroras glow when solar particles hit air
latest.script_key: auroras start with charged particles from the sun watch the moving color because charged particles follow earth s magnetic field toward the upper atmosphere when they hit oxygen and nitrogen the air releases light the sky is reacting to space weather which rare sky event next

latest.title: Rivers carve bends by stealing from one bank
latest.script_key: rivers move sideways while they flow watch the outside of the bend because faster water cuts that bank while slower water drops sediment inside the curve over time the whole river shifts across the landscape a bend is motion written in mud which water shape next now the river bend at the start makes sense
